message:
feat: CI action bumps, release profile tuning, edge-case fixes and extensive test coverage

CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml):
- Bump codecov/codecov-action from v4 to v5.
- Bump actions/setup-python from v5 to v6 across all four job matrices
  (coverage, fuzz, windows, benchmark).

Build (Cargo.toml):
- Add per-crate release profile override for ftui-extras: opt-level = 3
  to benefit VFX-heavy rasterizer inner loops from SIMD, loop unrolling,
  and aggressive inlining while the workspace default stays size-tuned.

Beads issue tracker (.beads/issues.jsonl):
- Add new issue bd-1nn7a (resize_sla edge-case tests, in_progress).
- Close bd-1uzxz (conformal_alert edge-case tests, was countmin_sketch).
- Add new issue bd-237l7 (ftui-web edge-case correctness fixes, in_progress).

frankenterm-web (frame_harness.rs):
- Replace 4 redundant .clone() calls with Copy semantics on Copy types
  (FrameResult, FrameTimeHistogram, GeometrySnapshot, InteractionSnapshot).
- Use struct literal with ..Default::default() instead of mutable field
  assignment in interaction_snapshot_effective_shaping_engine test.

ftui-core (inline_mode.rs):
- Add unicode_width dependency import for overlay log sanitization.
- New sanitize_overlay_log_line(): truncates log text to terminal width,
  strips control characters and newlines to prevent cursor motion injection
  and UI region scribbling from multi-line or wide-character log messages.
- Erase line before writing overlay log to prevent stale content bleed.
- Add config validation guard in present_ui() that rejects invalid
  configurations early with io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput.
- Refactor present_ui() render/restore lifecycle: cursor restore and
  sync-end are now guaranteed even when the render callback fails,
  preventing terminal state corruption on render errors.
- Add 5 new tests: overlay log truncation (single-line, wide chars,
  exact fit), render error state restoration, and invalid config rejection.

ftui-extras (doom/renderer.rs):
- Auto-resize DoomRenderer when framebuffer dimensions mismatch, avoiding
  panics or stale column clip arrays on dynamic resize.
- Add test_render_adapts_to_framebuffer_dimensions regression test.

ftui-extras (live.rs):
- Consolidate refresh_stop + refresh_handle into a single RefreshThread
  struct behind one Mutex, eliminating a race window where the stop flag
  and join handle could disagree.
- Move stop_refresh_thread() call before the started flag check in stop()
  so auto-refresh threads are always cleaned up even if start() was never
  called (or was called only for refresh).
- Prevent self-join deadlock by checking thread ID before join.
- Use unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) on poisoned mutexes for
  robustness after panics.
- Add test: stop_stops_refresh_thread_even_when_not_started verifies the
  callback ceases and the thread handle is cleared.

ftui-extras (plasma.rs):
- Replace debug_assert_eq! with a runtime early-return guard for
  output/context length mismatch, preventing undefined behavior in
  release builds when lengths drift during resize races.
- Add length_mismatch_is_ignored_without_mutating_output test.

ftui-harness (flicker_detection.rs):
- Replace redundant .clone() with Copy on Severity (already Copy).

ftui-render (diff.rs):
- Add 930+ lines of edge-case tests (bd-27b0k) covering:
  BufferDiff::full zero/single/row-major dims, runs_into correctness,
  ChangeRun traits, TileDiffConfig defaults/builders, TileDiffFallback
  as_str variants, TileDiffBuilder fallbacks and reuse, TileParams,
  TileDiffStats, tile size clamping, BufferDiff Default/Debug/Clone,
  compute_into/compute_dirty_into reuse, last_tile_stats, tile_config_mut,
  row scan boundaries at 31/32/33, DenseCells threshold boundary,
  compute path switching, dimension mismatch panics, attribute-only
  changes, corner-cell detection, and full trait coverage.

ftui-web (lib.rs):
- Replace hardcoded ATTR_LINK_ID_MAX with CellAttrs::LINK_ID_MAX constant.
- Add bounds check in diff_to_patches: stale diffs from resize fall back
  to a full-buffer patch instead of panicking.
- Add 2 tests: stale diff fallback, max link ID preservation.

ftui-web (session_record.rs):
- Apply json_unescape() to key code strings during parse_event_json(),
  fixing round-trip failures for keys like '"' and '\\'.
- Refactor test helpers to use and_then/expect and let-else patterns.
- Add key_event_json_round_trip_unescapes_code test.

ftui-widgets (command_palette/mod.rs):
- Add tracing::callsite::rebuild_interest_cache() calls gated behind
  #[cfg(test)] before tracing emission points to prevent stale interest
  caching from causing test-only tracing assertions to flake.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1nac.6","title":"Undo/Redo E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of command history and undo/redo functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Basic Undo/Redo (undo_basic_e2e.rs)
- Single action undo
- Redo after undo
- Multiple sequential undos
- Redo cleared on new action

### 2. Command Merging (undo_merge_e2e.rs)
- Consecutive typing merges
- Merge timeout boundary
- Non-mergeable commands
- Partial merge scenarios

### 3. Transactions (undo_transaction_e2e.rs)
- Transaction groups undo atomically
- Nested transaction handling
- Transaction rollback on error
- Interleaved transactions

### 4. Widget Integration (undo_widget_e2e.rs)
- TextInput undo (char, word, line)
- TreeView expand/collapse undo
- Table sort/filter undo
- Cross-widget history

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Command execution with type and description
- DEBUG: Merge decisions and reasons
- TRACE: History stack state after each operation
- Audit: Complete command history for debugging

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/undo_redo/

## State Verification
- History depth never exceeds limit
- Memory usage stable over time
- Command descriptions human-readable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Undo/Redo E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-1nn0","title":"Add recovery mechanism with configurable attempts","description":"# Add recovery mechanism with configurable attempts

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). After a widget fails, we should try to recover when conditions change (e.g., model updates).

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Configuration for error boundary behavior
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct ErrorBoundaryConfig {
    /// Maximum recovery attempts before giving up
    pub max_recovery_attempts: u32,
    
    /// Delay between recovery attempts (in frames)
    pub recovery_delay_frames: u32,
    
    /// Whether to log errors
    pub log_errors: bool,
    
    /// Custom fallback widget factory
    pub fallback_factory: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&CapturedError) -> Box<dyn Widget>>>,
    
    /// Whether to try recovery on model update
    pub recover_on_model_update: bool,
}

impl Default for ErrorBoundaryConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            max_recovery_attempts: 3,
            recovery_delay_frames: 5,
            log_errors: true,
            fallback_factory: None,
            recover_on_model_update: true,
        }
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    /// Attempt to recover from error state
    /// Call this when model changes or after delay
    pub fn try_recover(&mut self) -> bool {
        match &mut self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error) => {
                if self.config.max_recovery_attempts > 0 {
                    tracing::info!(
                        widget = self.name,
                        attempt = 1,
                        "Starting recovery attempt"
                    );
                    self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering {
                        attempts: 1,
                        last_error: error.clone(),
                    };
                    true
                } else {
                    false
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { attempts, last_error } => {
                if *attempts < self.config.max_recovery_attempts {
                    *attempts += 1;
                    tracing::info!(
                        widget = self.name,
                        attempt = *attempts,
                        max = self.config.max_recovery_attempts,
                        "Continuing recovery attempt"
                    );
                    true
                } else {
                    // Give up permanently
                    self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(last_error.clone());
                    tracing::warn!(
                        widget = self.name,
                        "Recovery failed after max attempts"
                    );
                    false
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => true,
        }
    }
    
    /// Reset to healthy state (force recovery)
    pub fn reset(&mut self) {
        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy;
        tracing::info!(widget = self.name, "Error boundary reset");
    }
    
    /// Signal that model has updated (may trigger recovery)
    pub fn on_model_update(&mut self) {
        if self.config.recover_on_model_update && self.is_failed() {
            self.try_recover();
        }
    }
    
    /// Get recovery status
    pub fn recovery_status(&self) -> RecoveryStatus {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => RecoveryStatus::Healthy,
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(_) => RecoveryStatus::Failed,
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { attempts, .. } => {
                RecoveryStatus::Recovering {
                    attempts: *attempts,
                    max_attempts: self.config.max_recovery_attempts,
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

/// Recovery status for external monitoring
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum RecoveryStatus {
    Healthy,
    Failed,
    Recovering { attempts: u32, max_attempts: u32 },
}
```

## Recovery Triggers
1. **Manual**: Call try_recover() explicitly
2. **Model update**: Automatic when config.recover_on_model_update
3. **Force reset**: Call reset() to immediately clear error

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ErrorBoundaryConfig with all options
- [ ] try_recover() increments attempts
- [ ] Max attempts enforced
- [ ] reset() forces healthy state
- [ ] on_model_update() triggers recovery
- [ ] RecoveryStatus for monitoring","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:18.791599230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:29:50.166964103Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:29:50.166938815Z","close_reason":"Recovery mechanism already implemented in ErrorBoundaryState with try_recover(max_attempts), Recovering state, attempts counter, and configurable max_recovery_attempts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nn0","depends_on_id":"bd-1fpo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1un","title":"Document Terminal Compatibility Matrix","description":"# Terminal Compatibility Matrix Documentation

## Background
Plan Chapter 16 provides a detailed compatibility matrix. This needs to be documented for users to understand what features work where.

## Compatibility Matrix (from Plan)

| Feature | Kitty | WezTerm | Alacritty | Ghostty | iTerm2 | GNOME Term | Win Term |
|---------|-------|---------|-----------|---------|--------|------------|----------|
| True Color | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Sync Output | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| OSC 8 Links | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ |
| Kitty Keyboard | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Kitty Graphics | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Sixel | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Focus Events | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Bracketed Paste | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

## Feature Details

### True Color (24-bit RGB)
- Detection: COLORTERM=truecolor or COLORTERM=24bit
- Fallback: 256-color or 16-color palette
- Most modern terminals support this

### Synchronized Output (DEC 2026)
- Purpose: Atomic frame updates, flicker prevention
- Detection: Known terminal identification
- Fallback: Buffered writes still help
- ftui activates when detected

### OSC 8 Hyperlinks
- Purpose: Clickable links in terminal
- Format: `\x1b]8;;URL\x1b\\text\x1b]8;;\x1b\\`
- Windows Terminal: Not supported
- Most Linux/macOS terminals support

### Kitty Keyboard Protocol
- Purpose: Enhanced key event information
- Provides: press/repeat/release, modifiers
- Limited adoption outside Kitty ecosystem
- Feature-gated in ftui

### Scroll Region (DECSTBM)
- Purpose: Inline mode optimization
- Support: Nearly universal
- Quirks: tmux/screen passthrough issues
- ftui uses as optimization, not requirement

## Multiplexer Notes

### tmux
- Passthrough: Requires `\x1bPtmux;...\x1b\\`
- Sync output: Blocked by default
- OSC 8: Needs recent version + config
- Detection: TMUX env var

### screen
- Limited modern feature support
- No sync output passthrough
- Basic ANSI works fine
- Detection: STY env var

### Zellij
- Better passthrough than tmux/screen
- Native terminal support varies
- Detection: ZELLIJ env var

## Windows Considerations

### Windows Terminal
- True color: ✓
- Mouse: ✓
- OSC 8: ✗
- Sync output: ✗
- Raw mode: Via Crossterm

### ConEmu/Cmder
- Limited feature set
- True color: ✓ (newer versions)
- ANSI support varies by version

### cmd.exe / PowerShell (legacy)
- Very limited ANSI support
- Windows 10+ improved support
- Use Windows Terminal for best experience

## Inline Mode Notes
- Works everywhere (no special requirements)
- Cursor save/restore: Universal
- Scroll region: Nearly universal (optimization only)
- Sync output: Nice-to-have (not required)

## Recommended Test Matrix
For CI, test against:
1. Kitty (most features)
2. WezTerm (cross-platform)
3. Alacritty (popular, minimal)
4. Plain Linux VT (baseline)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Matrix table documented
- [ ] Feature descriptions complete
- [ ] Multiplexer notes included
- [ ] Windows section clear
- [ ] Inline mode notes present
- [ ] Test matrix recommended

 {"id":"bd-1vb18","title":"Fix emoji-triggered border corruption in demo showcase (keep emojis)","description":"Problem
- Demo showcase has border misalignment/corruption in File Browser and Markdown screens when emojis are present. Emojis must remain.

Likely Causes
1) Several screens try to "clear" the full area using Paragraph::new("") + bg style, but Paragraph only paints style and does not clear cell content. With Flex gaps between panels, stale border characters from prior frames/screens can bleed into those gaps and appear as misaligned borders.
2) Buffer::print_text_clipped (ftui-render) advances by grapheme_width() but may only write the first char for multi-codepoint graphemes (e.g. VS16 sequences like "⚙️"), leaving holes that can preserve stale border chars.

Fix
- Clear demo screen root areas with actual space cells (bg=surface) so gaps are deterministically blank.
- Make Buffer::print_text_clipped fill any extra width when falling back to first char so no holes remain (and add a regression test).

Acceptance
- File Browser and Markdown screens render without stray/misaligned borders in gaps.
- Emojis remain enabled in file browser icons and unicode showcase.
- cargo fmt/check/clippy/test pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:01:09.704771751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:34:21.706728130Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:34:21.706701631Z","close_reason":"Completed (emoji border corruption fixed: VS16 width policy + buffer filler + empty paragraph clears)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["borders","demo-showcase","emoji","render","text"]}

 {"id":"bd-22ml6","title":"Docs: help overlay text sync","description":"Goal
- Ensure on-screen help overlay and docs match the keybinding map.

 {"id":"bd-22q","title":"Implement Theme system with semantic color slots","description":"PHASE 4 - THEME SYSTEM

## Background
A Theme provides semantic color slots that map to actual colors. This enables consistent styling and easy theme switching (light/dark mode, custom themes).

## Technical Requirements

### Semantic Color Slots
pub struct Theme {
    // Primary UI
    pub primary: AdaptiveColor,
    pub secondary: AdaptiveColor,
    pub accent: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Backgrounds
    pub background: AdaptiveColor,
    pub surface: AdaptiveColor,
    pub overlay: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Text
    pub text: AdaptiveColor,
    pub text_muted: AdaptiveColor,
    pub text_subtle: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Semantic
    pub success: AdaptiveColor,
    pub warning: AdaptiveColor,
    pub error: AdaptiveColor,
    pub info: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Borders
    pub border: AdaptiveColor,
    pub border_focused: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Selection
    pub selection_bg: AdaptiveColor,
    pub selection_fg: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Scrollbar
    pub scrollbar_track: AdaptiveColor,
    pub scrollbar_thumb: AdaptiveColor,
}

### Adaptive Colors
pub enum AdaptiveColor {
    Fixed(Color),
    Adaptive { light: Color, dark: Color },
}

impl AdaptiveColor {
    pub fn resolve(&self, is_dark: bool) -> Color {
        match self {
            Self::Fixed(c) => *c,
            Self::Adaptive { light, dark } => {
                if is_dark { *dark } else { *light }
            }
        }
    }
}

### Built-in Theme Presets
pub mod themes {
    pub fn default() -> Theme;      // Sensible defaults
    pub fn dark() -> Theme;         // Dark mode
    pub fn light() -> Theme;        // Light mode
    pub fn nord() -> Theme;         // Nord color scheme
    pub fn dracula() -> Theme;      // Dracula colors
    pub fn solarized_dark() -> Theme;
    pub fn solarized_light() -> Theme;
}

### Theme Detection
impl Theme {
    /// Detect dark mode from environment/terminal
    pub fn detect_dark_mode() -> bool {
        // Check COLORFGBG for light/dark background
        // Check OSC 11 background query (if supported)
        // Default: assume dark
    }
}

### Theme Resolution
Widgets reference theme colors:
  let style = Style::new().fg(theme.text).bg(theme.surface);

### Runtime Theme Switching
- Theme stored in Context or Model
- All widgets receive theme reference
- Switching triggers full redraw

## Tests Required
- [ ] All semantic slots have sensible defaults
- [ ] Adaptive color resolution correct
- [ ] Built-in presets complete
- [ ] Dark mode detection works
- [ ] Theme switching triggers redraw

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ semantic color slots
- [ ] Light/dark adaptive colors
- [ ] 5+ built-in presets
- [ ] Detection heuristics
- [ ] Theme switching API

- Optimization must be data-driven: measure → pick hotspot → change one lever → prove identical output.

- Maintain strict isomorphism: identical inputs produce identical outputs (hashes must match).

Scope / Work Items

  - List top 5 hotspots (function + line).
  - For each: Impact (1-5), Confidence (1-5), Effort (1-5), Score = (Impact×Confidence)/Effort.
- Select only Score ≥ 2.0 items for implementation.
- Apply a single, isolated optimization per change (cache, reuse, algorithmic improvement, etc.).
- After each change: reprofile and verify hashes with the golden registry.
- Document an isomorphism proof per change (ordering, tie-breakers, RNG/time).

Constraints
- No quality/resolution degradation, no visual output changes.
- Deterministic output required; hashes must be unchanged unless explicitly blessed.
- Avoid adding new files unless absolutely necessary; prefer existing logs/tests.

Success Target (Performance)
- Achieve a clearly measurable improvement: ≥30% reduction in p95 total frame time for at least two heavy effects (e.g., plasma/metaballs/doom/quake) under the harness.

Notes
